name: Charged Particle Motion in Magnetic Fields
description: Calculate trajectories, radii, and periods for charged particles moving in uniform magnetic fields
identification_signal: Charged particle (electron, proton, ion) moving perpendicular to magnetic field with circular path

strategy.step_1: Equate magnetic force to centripetal force: qvB = mv²/r
strategy.step_4: Use right-hand rule for direction (reverse for negative charges)

phase_1_recognition.purpose: Recognize circular motion in B-field problems

front.text: Proton enters uniform B-field perpendicular to velocity

back.title: Circular Motion in B-Field
back.explanation: F = qvB provides centripetal force. Path is circular with r = mv/qB.

steps.title: Force Balance
steps.instruction: Magnetic force provides centripetal acceleration: qvB = mv²/r
steps.why: Force is perpendicular to velocity, causing circular motion

steps.title: Solve for Parameters
steps.instruction: Radius r = mv/qB. Period T = 2πm/qB (independent of v!)

checkpoint.question: Does period depend on velocity?
checkpoint.answer: No! T depends only on m, q, and B

solution.verification: Fast electron, small radius in moderate B-field ✓

what_mastery_means: Know that T is independent of v
Understand helical motion when v not ⊥ B
